project_reference.core_research_question: Can any hydride or hydride-derived pathway supported by first-principles evidence retain superconductivity at ambient pressure, or after pressure quench, and if not, which experimentally anchored broader benchmark route should replace that search as the repo's next high-confidence candidate?
project_reference.current_focus: Phase 10 - Experimental Benchmark Pivot Beyond Hydrides

scope.question: Can any hydride or hydride-derived pathway supported by first-principles evidence retain superconductivity at ambient pressure, or after pressure quench, and if not, do current constraints rule out room-temperature consumer viability within this conventional materials route?
in_scope: Pressure release, metastability, and quenchability of CsInH3-class phases
Ambient-pressure and near-ambient hydrides or hydride-derived frameworks
Alloying and structural redesign around MXH3, Mg2XH6-like, and hydride-unit/clathrate families
Practical viability metrics: operating pressure, synthesis pressure, ambient stability, barriers, cost, toxicity
Real DFPT/EPW/SSCHA validation targets for any candidate advanced as practically relevant
out_of_scope: Device engineering, interconnect design, or packaging workflows
Experimental synthesis claims not backed by direct evidence
Exhaustive AIRSS/CALYPSO searches beyond current compute scope
Unconventional pairing as the main program for this milestone
unresolved_questions: Which ambient-leaning or hydride-derived family can replace the failed `CsInH3`-class decompression route?
Which candidate families best balance Tc, operating pressure, synthesis pressure, quenchability, and materials practicality?
Does the 2025-2026 literature already force a pivot away from room-temperature consumer claims in conventional hydrides?
Is any pressure-quench route more credible than a fully stable ambient hydride route after the negative `CsInH3`-class verdict?

user_asserted_anchors: Do not treat synthesis pressure as operating pressure
Room-temperature consumer viability requires ambient retention, not just lower pressure synthesis
known_good_baselines: v1.0 benchmark pipeline: H3S error 10.5%, LaH10 error 10.6%
CsInH3 SSCHA result: Tc = 214 K at 3 GPa, E_hull = 6 meV/atom
context_gaps: Ambient-pressure quenchability of CsInH3 is unknown
Real DFPT+EPW alpha^2F for CsInH3 is still missing
Kinetic decomposition barriers during decompression are still missing
crucial_inputs: Exact separation of operating pressure vs synthesis pressure for all cited systems
Barrier-aware decompression analysis for CsInH3-class phases
Real EPW/SSCHA validation for any candidate advanced as practical

formulations: DFT/DFPT/EPW/SSCHA for conventional superconductivity in candidate materials
Decompression phonon and enthalpy path analysis for pressure release
Barrier and metastability analysis for quenchability
Literature-grounded practical viability ranking with explicit pressure accounting
forbidden_estimator_families: Ambient Tc extrapolated from high-pressure Tc without stability evidence
Treating synthesis pressure as operating pressure
allowed_fit_families: Explicit pressure-dependent fits only when anchored by computed stable points
forbidden_fit_families: Post-hoc mu* tuning to hit target Tc
Free extrapolation of Tc(P) beyond the stable pressure window
stop_and_rethink_conditions: Stable ambient hydride routes remain near or below the known conventional ambient ceiling and no credible quench path survives decompression
All decompression paths for CsInH3-class phases develop strong instability or barrierless decomposition before ambient pressure
Any consumer-viability claim depends on synthetic alpha^2F or unverified extrapolation

observables.name: Superconducting critical temperature Tc
observables.definition: Tc from converged Eliashberg calculations or directly cited primary-source measurements

observables.name: Operating pressure
observables.definition: Pressure required for the superconducting phase to exist during operation

observables.name: Synthesis pressure
observables.definition: Pressure used during synthesis or preparation before any decompression or quench step

observables.name: Quenchability / ambient retention verdict
observables.definition: Barrier-aware assessment of whether a phase can survive pressure release to ambient conditions

observables.name: Barrier against decomposition or symmetry lowering
observables.definition: Representative kinetic barrier for a pressure-release pathway

observables.name: Ambient thermodynamic and dynamic stability
observables.definition: Combined hull and phonon verdict at or near 0 GPa

claims.statement: The project cleanly distinguishes stable ambient, metastable ambient, pressure-quenched, and low-pressure-only pathways relevant to practical superconductivity.
At least one CsInH3-class phase receives an explicit evidence-based quenchability verdict under decompression.
At least one pathway reaches either ambient pressure with Tc >= 77 K or a credible pressure-quench route retaining superconductivity above 150 K.

deliverables.description: Matrix of candidate pathways with explicit synthesis pressure, operating pressure, Tc, stability class, and evidence quality
Barrier-aware decompression verdict for CsInH3 and one derivative
Ranked shortlist of ambient-leaning or quenchable candidate families and compositions
Real EPW / SSCHA validation package for the top practical pathway
Final statement on whether conventional hydrides still support a consumer-hardware path

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-practical-map
acceptance_tests.procedure: Audit every cited pathway and repo candidate for separate synthesis-pressure and operating-pressure fields
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No pathway merges synthesis pressure and operating pressure into a single practicality claim

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-quenchability
acceptance_tests.procedure: Use decompression energetics, phonons, and barriers to determine whether the phase can plausibly survive to ambient pressure
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The project produces an explicit quenchability verdict with barrier-aware evidence

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-practical-candidate
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check whether any validated pathway reaches ambient pressure with Tc >= 77 K or a credible quench-retained route above 150 K
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: At least one pathway satisfies one of the two practical thresholds without relying on forbidden proxies

references.why_it_matters: Defines the current best result and explicitly states that ambient pressure and room-temperature operation are unproven
Makes the synthesis-pressure vs operating-pressure distinction concrete for the current top candidate

references.locator: Gao et al., The maximum Tc of conventional superconductors at ambient pressure (2025)
references.why_it_matters: Provides the strongest current skepticism prior against room-temperature conventional superconductivity at ambient pressure

references.locator: Yanagizawa et al., stable ambient-pressure superconducting hydrides in GNoME (2026)
references.why_it_matters: Defines the current stable-ambient hydride baseline and shows how far the field remains from room temperature

references.locator: Guo et al., Dynamic high-temperature superconductor at ambient pressure (2025)
references.why_it_matters: Shows that non-equilibrium pressure history can produce ambient superconductivity in a hydride-derived framework

references.locator: Ambient-pressure 151-K superconductivity in HgBa2Ca2Cu3O8+δ via pressure quench (2026)
references.why_it_matters: Demonstrates that pressure-quench metastability can retain very high Tc at ambient pressure in a nearby materials context

references.locator: Ambient-pressure hydride family predictions above 80 K in Mg2XH6-like systems (2024)
references.why_it_matters: Best-known stable-ambient conventional hydride direction still falls well below room temperature

references.locator: Hydride-unit filled B-C clathrate design strategy for ambient-pressure high-temperature superconductors (2024)
references.why_it_matters: Motivates moving beyond simple perovskites toward more complex chemically precompressed frameworks

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-practical-map
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Presenting a lower synthesis pressure as if it were the operating pressure of the superconducting phase
forbidden_proxies.reason: This is the central practical ambiguity that makes loaded-pressure results look more useful than they are

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-practical-candidate
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Final practical-path claims based only on synthetic alpha^2F or calibrated spectral models
forbidden_proxies.reason: Synthetic spectra are acceptable for scoping, not for a decisive practical viability claim

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-quenchability
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Calling a decompressed phase ambient-viable when it shows unresolved imaginary modes or barrierless decomposition
forbidden_proxies.reason: Ambient utility requires survival after release, not just hope that release might work

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-practical-candidate
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Extrapolating a room-temperature consumer path from low-pressure Tc without ambient evidence or without respecting known ambient ceilings
forbidden_proxies.reason: This turns a scientific low-pressure result into an unsupported product claim

weakest_anchors: Ambient retention in hydrides themselves remains largely unproven
Synthetic alpha^2F still underlies some legacy candidate rankings
unvalidated_assumptions: CsInH3 can survive decompression without catastrophic symmetry lowering
Alloying can improve ambient stability without destroying hydrogen-dominated modes
Non-equilibrium pressure history can be translated from adjacent systems to hydrides
competing_explanations: Conventional hydrides may simply saturate far below room temperature at ambient pressure
The best practical route may require leaving the current material family altogether
disconfirming_observations: All decompression paths fail before ambient pressure
Stable ambient or quenchable candidates remain below the practical thresholds defined in VALD-02

intermediate_results: v1.0 archived: CsInH3 remains the best repo result at `Tc = 214 K` and `3 GPa`
Ambient pressure after synthesis remains unestablished for CsInH3
Stable ambient conventional hydrides in the recent literature remain far below room temperature
Pressure-quench / metastability is now the main escape route worth testing
Phase 07 executed: the `CsInH3` class is unlikely to retain a superconducting ambient-pressure phase after decompression
`CsInH3` remains useful as a low-pressure benchmark, not as a practical ambient-route candidate
Phase 08 executed: no candidate survives as a decisive mixed-evidence ambient winner in the shared `0-5 GPa` screen
`RbPH3` is now the Phase 09 negative-validation primary and `KB3C3` is the framework benchmark
Phase 08 triggers a no-go on consumer-hardware framing unless Phase 09 overturns it with stronger evidence
Phase 09 planned: validation is now locked to `CsInH3` baseline, `RbPH3` primary, `KB3C3` benchmark, and an explicit no-synthetic-final evidence gate
Phase 09 executed: no route passes the shared high-fidelity gate, so v2.0 closes with no credible consumer path inside the present conventional hydride route
Phase 10 executed: `HgBa2Ca2Cu3O8+delta` via pressure quench is now the repo's strongest confidence-ranked benchmark candidate, but it remains 149 K below room temperature
open_questions: What parameter window and reproducibility story actually govern the `Hg1223` pressure-quench route?
Which broader comparator should be treated as second priority after `Hg1223`: `SmNiO2` or a more practical ambient baseline such as `MgB2`?
Is any future route likely to close the remaining `149 K` room-temperature gap without abandoning experimental credibility?

decisions.summary: Start milestone `v2.0 Ambient Retention and Practical Viability`
Separate synthesis pressure from operating pressure in every future claim
Treat quenchability and decompression stability as first-class requirements
Started milestone v2.0: Ambient Retention and Practical Viability
decisions.rationale: Archived the v1.0 negative-result milestone and pivoted the live roadmap toward ambient retention, quenchability, and practical operating conditions

decisions.summary: Phase 06 research: prioritize ambient retention and metastability over further pressure-supported Tc optimization
decisions.rationale: The 2024-2026 literature does not support room-temperature consumer viability for conventional ambient hydrides. CsInH3 remains the best low-pressure hydride result at 3 GPa, but ambient retention is unproven. The most credible next work is decompression/barrier analysis for CsInH3 plus targeted study of ambient-leaning families such as RbPH3, Mg2IrH6, and hydride-derived clathrates.

decisions.summary: Phase 06 executed: CsInH3 remains Phase 07 priority, RbPH3 becomes top hydride ambient target, and consumer framing now requires >=100 K ambient-retained evidence
decisions.rationale: Phase 06 artifacts now separate synthesis pressure from operating pressure, map metastability and quench routes, and rank pathways by practical viability. The immediate next work is decompression and barrier analysis for CsInH3, with RbPH3 as the leading ambient-leaning hydride comparator. If no path survives with P_op = 0 GPa, Tc >= 100 K, and nontrivial retention confidence, the milestone should pivot from consumer-hardware language to a low-pressure and quench-enabled superconductivity map.

decisions.summary: Phase 07 planning: use RbInH3 as the same-family derivative and reserve RbPH3 for Phase 08 comparator work
decisions.rationale: The decompression verdict should test the CsInH3 perovskite class under one coherent logic. RbInH3 shares the MXH3 Pm-3m design and already has repo-local stability and Tc anchors, so it is the right derivative for Phase 07. RbPH3 remains valuable, but as an ambient-leaning literature comparator for Phase 08 rather than as a same-family decompression control.

decisions.summary: Phase 07 executed: CsInH3-class ambient retention is unlikely, so keep `CsInH3` as a low-pressure benchmark and move the practical search to Phase 08 families
decisions.rationale: The decompression path fails before ambient pressure, the ambient cubic endpoint is not locally protected, and the same-family `RbInH3` comparison does not rescue the class. Practical follow-up should move toward ambient-leaning hydrides, contradiction-tracked `Mg2IrH6`, and hydride-derived clathrate/framework routes rather than plain `MXH3` retention optimism.

decisions.summary: Phase 08 planning: shortlist `RbPH3`, a limited perovskite-side bridge bucket, contradiction-tracked `Mg2XH6` members, and `KB3C3` / `KRbB6C6` frameworks for a common `0-5 GPa` screen
decisions.rationale: The next candidate search should stay downstream of the negative CsInH3-class verdict. `RbPH3` remains the primary hydride ambient target, `Mg2IrH6` must stay contradiction-tracked rather than headline-driven, and framework routes deserve direct comparison because they may offer a more honest ambient path even at lower `Tc`.

decisions.summary: Phase 08 executed: no decisive ambient survivor emerges, so Phase 09 should negatively validate `RbPH3` against a `KB3C3` benchmark rather than force a practical winner
decisions.rationale: The shared `0-5 GPa` screen ends with zero survivors, `Mg2IrH6` stays contradiction-tracked, the NH4-filled clathrates are rejected on prior local hull failures, and the ranking triggers a no-go on consumer-hardware language.

decisions.summary: Phase 09 planning: lock negative-validation roles and require real EPW / anharmonic evidence before any practical pass
decisions.rationale: `CsInH3` stays the baseline control, `RbPH3` is the hydride-side primary, `KB3C3` is the benchmark, and the phase must end in either a validated pass or an explicit no-go without consumer-language drift.

decisions.summary: Phase 09 executed: no route passes the shared high-fidelity practical gate, so v2.0 closes negatively and the next milestone should pivot to experimentally anchored ambient or pressure-quench benchmarks outside the current hydride-only frame
decisions.rationale: `CsInH3` remains the low-pressure benchmark, `RbPH3` is blocked, `KB3C3` remains benchmark-only, and `HgBa2Ca2Cu3O8+delta` becomes the strongest broader benchmark candidate.

decisions.summary: Added Phase 10: Experimental benchmark pivot beyond hydrides
decisions.rationale: Continue beyond the v2.0 hydride no-go by auditing the strongest experimentally anchored ambient or pressure-quench benchmark route

decisions.summary: Phase 10 executed: `HgBa2Ca2Cu3O8+delta` is now the top confidence-ranked benchmark candidate, while `MgB2` is the practical ambient floor and `SmNiO2` the main ambient-oxide comparator
decisions.rationale: The repo now has a candidate it can actually be confident in, but not a room-temperature consumer-hardware solution.

convention_lock.fourier_convention: QE plane-wave: `psi_nk = e^{ikr} u_nk`; asymmetric `1/Omega` normalization

custom_conventions.dos_convention: `N_F` per spin per cell (EPW); QE `dos.x` gives total for both spins
custom_conventions.phonon_imaginary: Negative frequency = imaginary mode; threshold `-5 cm^-1`
custom_conventions.eliashberg_method: Isotropic Eliashberg on Matsubara axis; Allen-Dynes cross-check only unless upgraded explicitly

pending_todos: Plan the next phase around `Hg1223` pressure-quench parameter windows and reproducibility
Compare `SmNiO2` against `MgB2` as the strongest secondary benchmark family
blockers: The top benchmark candidate is still 149 K below room temperature
Pressure-quench reproducibility and scale-up remain unclear even for the strongest benchmark route

session.stopped_at: Phase 10 executed; `HgBa2Ca2Cu3O8+delta` selected as the top confidence-ranked benchmark candidate
